task_completion.visual_evidence: An editor (like Android Studio, VS Code, nano, or vim) is open showing the contents of the 'app/build.gradle' file.

task_completion.description: Create a staging build type that inherits from debug
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'staging {' is visible inside the 'buildTypes' block, and it contains 'initWith debug' (or similar Groovy syntax) to inherit from the debug build type.
The text 'applicationIdSuffix ".staging"' (or with single quotes) is visible inside the staging build type block.
The text 'versionNameSuffix "-STAGING"' (or with single quotes) is visible inside the staging build type block.
The text 'buildConfigField "String", "BASE_URL", "\"https://staging.api.unscramble.com\""' (or equivalent Groovy syntax with properly escaped quotes) is visible inside the staging build type block.

A 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message is visible in the terminal or IDE build console after executing a build command specifically for the staging variant (e.g., running './gradlew assembleStaging' or selecting the staging build variant in Android Studio and building).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a real text editor or IDE to modify the file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an IDE (like Android Studio) or a terminal text editor (like nano/vim) being used to edit the file, rather than just echo commands or text manipulation scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the build was actually executed by Gradle
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine Gradle build output with execution times, task lists, daemon status, or IDE progress indicators, not just a manually printed success message.

integrity.description: Verify the edits were made to the actual Unscramble project
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths in the editor, IDE project tree, or terminal prompt show the Unscramble project directory structure, ensuring a dummy file wasn't created elsewhere.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Unscramble app uses a Groovy-format build.gradle file, not Kotlin DSL (build.gradle.kts). The expected applicationIdSuffix is '.staging' and the expected versionNameSuffix is '-STAGING'.
